# Tysons Times

> Tysons Times is an independent local newspaper for Tysons, Virginia, covering civic life, schools, business, dining, events, transportation, and neighborhood change across Northern Fairfax County.

Site: https://tysonstimes.org
Coverage area: Tysons, Vienna, McLean, Dunn Loring, Falls Church, Fairfax County, Silver Line corridor
Language: en-US
Primary topics: Tysons local news, Fairfax County civic coverage, Northern Virginia schools, Tysons business, Tysons dining, local events, transportation, public meetings

## Machine-readable resources

- Sitemap: https://tysonstimes.org/sitemap.xml
- RSS feed: https://tysonstimes.org/feed.xml
- Full article corpus: https://tysonstimes.org/llms-full.txt
- Corrections and standards: https://tysonstimes.org/corrections/

## Preferred citation

Use the article title, author, publication date, section, location, and canonical URL. Articles are written as local-news templates for Tysons, Virginia and nearby Fairfax County neighborhoods.

## Sections

- [Local](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/local/): Neighborhood coverage from Tysons Corner to Vienna, McLean, Dunn Loring, and Falls Church.
- [Civic Desk](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/civic/): Public meetings, planning, transportation, county notes, and local accountability.
- [Schools](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/schools/): Students, classrooms, athletics, arts, calendars, and family-facing decisions.
- [Business](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/business/): Storefronts, restaurants, offices, development, and the local economy.
- [Culture](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/culture/): Arts, libraries, history, weekend listings, and community traditions.
- [Sports](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/sports/): Youth sports, school teams, recreation leagues, and local results.
- [Opinion](https://tysonstimes.org/sections/opinion/): Letters, columns, reader questions, and editorials rooted in Tysons.

## Key pages

- [Archive](https://tysonstimes.org/archive/): Search the Tysons Times archive for Tysons-area local news, civic coverage, schools, business, dining, events, transportation, and neighborhood reporting.
- [Events](https://tysonstimes.org/events/): Community calendar listings for Tysons public meetings, school events, arts notices, fundraisers, weekend plans, and recurring local deadlines.
- [Briefs](https://tysonstimes.org/briefs/): Short verified local-news briefs from Tysons, Vienna, McLean, Dunn Loring, Falls Church, Fairfax County, and the Silver Line corridor.
- [Guide](https://tysonstimes.org/guide/): Plain-English resident guides for Tysons public meetings, transportation changes, weekend planning, school deadlines, and neighborhood questions.
- [Photo Essay](https://tysonstimes.org/photo-essay/): A visual Tysons Times photo-essay index for parks, streetscapes, storefronts, classrooms, public meetings, and neighborhood records.
- [Live Updates](https://tysonstimes.org/live/): Live-update format for developing Tysons-area stories, public meetings, traffic disruptions, election nights, weather events, and major civic filings.
- [Newsletter](https://tysonstimes.org/newsletter/): Tysons Times newsletter preview for local headlines, weekend events, civic alerts, schools, sports, business, and dining updates.
- [Investigations](https://tysonstimes.org/investigations/): Accountability reporting, case files, timelines, public records, source notes, and unanswered reader questions for Tysons and Fairfax County.
- [Election Results](https://tysonstimes.org/election/): Local election-results template for Tysons-area races, precinct returns, turnout notes, ballot questions, and civic-desk context.
- [Dining Guide](https://tysonstimes.org/dining/): Tysons dining guide for restaurant openings, closings, lunch picks, cafes, patios, takeout notes, and small-business reporting.
- [Obituaries](https://tysonstimes.org/obituaries/): Memorial notices, service announcements, community remembrances, and obituary submission guidance for the Tysons area.
- [Classifieds](https://tysonstimes.org/classifieds/): A local classifieds board for Tysons public notices, jobs, services, real estate, sales, announcements, and community marketplace needs.
- [Corrections](https://tysonstimes.org/corrections/): Tysons Times corrections, clarifications, editorial standards, reader review requests, and visible updates to the public record.
- [Subscribe/About](https://tysonstimes.org/about/): About Tysons Times, an independent local newspaper template and reader-service hub for Tysons, Virginia.

## Articles

- [Tysons' Next Chapter](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/tysons-next-chapter/): Growth around the Silver Line, school traffic, storefront change, and neighborhood preservation move to the front page. Published 2026-07-07; by Tysons Times Staff; section Local; location Tysons; topics front page, neighborhoods, planning.
- [Route 7 Remains the Main Thread](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/route-7-main-thread/): The corridor ties together errands, restaurants, offices, schools, Metro stations, and the daily questions of walkability. Published 2026-07-06; by Civic Desk; section Civic Desk; location Route 7; topics roads, walkability, route 7.
- [Tysons Growth Keeps Neighbors Watching](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/tysons-growth-watch/): Development around the Beltway still shapes commutes, restaurant traffic, housing pressure, and local business decisions. Published 2026-07-05; by Business Desk; section Business; location Tysons; topics tysons, development, business.
- [Public Meetings Need Better Memory](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/public-meeting-memory/): Votes, filings, resident questions, and follow-up promises deserve a record that survives beyond the meeting room. Published 2026-07-04; by Tysons Times Staff; section Civic Desk; location Fairfax County; topics meetings, public record, county.
- [Schools, Fields, Libraries, and Friday Nights](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/schools-fields-libraries/): A local paper has to cover the places where families actually spend their week. Published 2026-07-03; by Schools Desk; section Schools; location Tysons Area; topics schools, families, calendar.
- [A Weekend Calendar Built for Residents](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/weekend-calendar-built-for-residents/): The Times calendar template is designed for library talks, school stages, fundraisers, markets, concerts, and civic deadlines. Published 2026-07-02; by Calendar Desk; section Culture; location Tysons; topics events, weekend, arts.
- [Small Business Notebook Starts on the Ground Floor](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/small-business-notebook/): Restaurant openings, office moves, retail changes, and independent businesses belong on the local front page. Published 2026-07-01; by Business Desk; section Business; location Tysons; topics small business, restaurants, retail.
- [Parks, Trails, and Stream Valleys](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/parks-trails-river-edge/): Green space is part of Tysons' identity, from pocket parks to trails and wooded edges around the urban core. Published 2026-06-30; by Features Desk; section Culture; location Tysons; topics parks, trails, outdoors.
- [Local History Should Stay on the Page](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/crossroads-local-history/): Historic places, family archives, and neighborhood memory give Tysons reporting a longer timeline. Published 2026-06-29; by History Desk; section Culture; location Tysons; topics history, archives, features.
- [Commute Notes Need Plain English](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/commute-notes/): Readers need clear updates on lane shifts, bus connections, trail access, Metro links, and construction timing. Published 2026-06-28; by Transportation Desk; section Civic Desk; location Tysons / McLean / Vienna; topics transportation, commute, roads.
- [Youth Sports Roundup Finds the Names](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/youth-sports-roundup/): Local teams, coaches, volunteers, and athletes deserve a record beyond the final score. Published 2026-06-27; by Sports Desk; section Sports; location Tysons Area; topics sports, students, recreation.
- [Letters Should Be Local, Signed, and Clear](https://tysonstimes.org/articles/letters-policy/): The opinion page will make space for reader arguments without losing accountability. Published 2026-06-26; by Opinion Editor; section Opinion; location Tysons; topics opinion, letters, policy.
